aliases: Beef Hot Dog with Cheese Sauce

english: Beef Hot Dog with Cheese Sauce

food_subgroup: Processed meat products — beef hot dog with toppings

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general hot dog entry; the Smoke Dog is a specific branded variant with cheese sauce, cherry peppers, and applewood-smoked bacon. Closest parent match used as no exact branded entry exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers hot dog sausage as a processed meat product. The full assembled sandwich with toppings is not separately coded; closest parent match applied to the primary protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept found for a branded beef hot dog sandwich with cheese sauce and bacon toppings.

allergens: soy (possible in sausage casing or bun)

serving.natural_language_portion: One Smoke Dog serving is approximately 200 g, comprising a beef frankfurter in a bun topped with cheese sauce, cherry peppers, and applewood-smoked bacon, as served at Shake Shack Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Hot dog, beef, plain on bun — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21120) adapted with cheese sauce and bacon toppings
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base values derived from USDA SR Legacy entry for beef hot dog on bun (NDB 21120, ~250 kcal/100g). Values adjusted upward for the addition of cheese sauce (~60 kcal/100g contribution) and applewood-smoked bacon (~540 kcal/100g contribution at ~10% weight fraction), yielding a blended composite estimate. Sodium elevated to reflect cheese sauce and cured bacon. Saturated fat elevated to reflect dairy cheese sauce and bacon fat. This is an adapted/estimated composite; exact Shake Shack nutritional data is not available in any approved primary source.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of bread-based mixed meals — analogous estimate from hot dog bun (white bread GI ~70) blended with protein and fat components reducing overall GI
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a beef hot dog with cheese sauce and bacon. The GI is estimated at 60 (medium) based on: (1) white bread / hot dog bun GI of approximately 70 per University of Sydney GI Database; (2) the substantial protein (12.5 g/100g) and fat (17 g/100g) content of the assembled product, which are well-established to attenuate postprandial glucose response; (3) analogous mixed meat-and-bread meal GI values in the range 45–65 from peer-reviewed literature (Wolever et al., Am J Clin Nutr). GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 40) / 100 = 24.0, using 200 g serving with 20 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~780 mg/100g; ~1560 mg per serving) from cured bacon, cheese sauce, and processed beef sausage
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this food; a single serving provides approximately 65–70% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, primarily from the combination of processed beef frankfurter, cured applewood-smoked bacon, and cheese sauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~60) with moderate carbohydrate load (~40 g per serving) from the hot dog bun, partially offset by high protein and fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this food cautiously; the glycemic load of approximately 24 per serving is moderate, and the high saturated fat and sodium content from bacon and cheese sauce add additional metabolic risk beyond glycemic impact alone.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~6.5 g/100g), trans fat (~0.3 g/100g), cholesterol (~55 mg/100g), and very high sodium (~780 mg/100g) from processed meat, cured bacon, and cheese sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This food is not recommended for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease; the combination of high saturated fat from cheese sauce and bacon, detectable trans fat from processed meat, and very high sodium significantly elevates LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk, and processed red meat consumption is independently associated with increased cardiovascular mortality per WHO and AHA guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Smoke Dog is a premium beef hot dog sandwich served at Shake Shack restaurants in Singapore, including locations at 89 Neil Road and One Fullerton. It features a 100% beef frankfurter nestled in a soft split-top bun, topped with a rich cheese sauce, tangy cherry peppers, and crispy applewood-smoked bacon. The flavour profile is savoury and smoky, with the sweetness of the cheese sauce balanced by the acidity of the cherry peppers and the saltiness of the cured bacon. The texture combines the snap of the grilled beef sausage with the softness of the bun and the crunch of the bacon. Nutritionally, the Smoke Dog is a calorie-dense, high-sodium, high-saturated-fat food. Per 100 g it provides approximately 285 kcal, 12.5 g protein, 17 g fat (of which 6.5 g is saturated), and 20 g carbohydrate. A standard serving of approximately 200 g delivers around 570 kcal and 1560 mg sodium, making it a significant contributor to daily sodium intake. The glycemic index is estimated at 60 (medium), but the glycemic load per serving is approximately 24, which is moderate to high. Clinically, this food is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to its very high sodium and saturated fat content from the processed meat, cured bacon, and cheese sauce. For people with Type 2 Diabetes, the moderate glycemic load combined with high saturated fat warrants careful portion control. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following halal or kosher diets without verification. Contains gluten (bun), dairy (cheese sauce), and possible soy allergens.

hot dog with bacon and cheese nutrition

confidence_reason: The Smoke Dog is a branded Shake Shack menu item. No exact nutritional data for this specific product is available in any approved primary source (USDA, HPB, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for beef hot dog on bun with composite adjustments for cheese sauce and applewood-smoked bacon toppings. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for white bread and mixed protein-fat meal attenuation. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (beef hot dog on bun, NDB 21120) with upward adjustments for cheese sauce and applewood-smoked bacon. No exact Shake Shack nutritional panel is available in any approved primary source. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 5. Trans fat of 0.3 g/100g is estimated from USDA data for processed beef sausage; exact value for this product is unknown. 6. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Halal status is unconfirmed; Shake Shack Singapore has not been verified as halal-certified in approved sources. 8. This food is classified under the Burgers cuisine tag from field survey data but is more precisely an American-style hot dog sandwich. 9. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore. 10. All -1 sentinel values represent nutrients not reported in the adapted USDA source and are documented per G3 requirements.
